title: Co-Design, Equity, and Accessible Participation · Product Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for co-design, equity, and accessible participation and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for co-design plan and equity impact record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence.
Budget format, assistive technology, language, schedule, compensation, connectivity, cognitive load, and support accommodations before recruitment.
Compare benefits, burdens, error rates, exclusion, recourse, and resource demands across relevant groups without inventing demographic certainty.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Participation Level · changed case · Quiz Participation Level
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. The quiz variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence.

Which action demonstrates Participation Level without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Participation Level · changed case · Quiz Participation Level.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose inform, consult, involve, collaborate, or shared-decision participation deliberately and communicate its actual influence. For Participation Level · changed case · Quiz Participation Level, keep Participation Level connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feedback Accountability
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Feedback Accountability: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Affected people are invited after scope is fixed, receive inaccessible materials, and never learn which input changed the decision. The quiz variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feedback Accountability.

For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feedback Accountability, keep Feedback Accountability connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Report what input changed, did not change, why, who decided, and how participants can challenge or withdraw.
